jd_verbose.txt.description: 40+ token JD covering Senior Full-Stack Engineer requirements. Designed to produce raw BM25 scores that exceed the old BM25_SATURATION_CAP=12.0, proving the cap was broken.

jd_terse.txt.description: 5-8 token JD. Proves the new normalization does not artificially deflate short JDs relative to their match density.

jd_medium.txt.description: 15-20 token JD. Used as the reference JD for the perfect-match saturation test and SCALE_FACTOR calibration.

candidate_dense.txt.description: Strong keyword overlap against all three JDs. Used in the JD-length independence test: should score within a 0.15 band across verbose/terse/medium JDs.

candidate_sparse.txt.description: Minimal content and overlap. Represents a weak/no-match candidate.

candidate_stuffer.txt.description: Repeats keywords from jd_verbose multiple times but actual experience is unrelated backend work. Tests that query-length normalization does not reward raw repetition.

candidate_perfect.txt.description: 100% keyword overlap with jd_medium. Used to calibrate SCALE_FACTOR: perfect match must yield normalized score in 0.80-0.85 range (not saturating at 1.0).

note: All fixtures are synthetic with no real PII. Designed to stress-test BM25 query-length normalization across JD length extremes and keyword-stuffing patterns.
